metrics.narrative: Mandy Huff articulated clear qualitative pain but no quantified value case yet. She described a 140-year-old company that grew through acquisition, now with 'a lot of legacy, smaller suppliers' across '45 DCs just in The United States' that 'like to operate independently', creating tail/maverick spend and visibility gaps (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). She framed centralizing indirect spend as how 'we get your value... our protection... deleverage and eliminate risk'. No cost, savings, headcount, or cycle-time figures were stated, so executive budget justification is not yet supportable. Quantifying tail-spend savings and visibility impact is a ranked next move.

champion.narrative: Mandy Huff is a developing champion. She proactively re-engaged Zycus from a positive 2019 Horizon-conference memory, joined two intro calls (26 May, 9 Jun), and stated she has 'aligned with my CPO' and wants 'introductions' (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). Her advocacy is genuine but unproven internally: she is new to the role (joined April 2026), candidly said 'I'm not sure what the opportunity would be here', and the orchestration area 'doesn't sit directly under my responsibility'. What has developed: an engaged, recurring contact who wants the spend-analytics demo and CPO introductions. What is still missing: evidence she can mobilize budget or the buying committee. Securing and equipping her, then multi-threading, is a ranked move.

competition.narrative: There is an unnamed incumbent S2P platform: Mandy said 'we do have a pretty substantial procurement tool' and 'we have contract management right now', but described both as underused and manual ('I don't think we do use it to its full capability... it's more of a manual process right now') (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). The rep also referenced intake-only rivals generically ('there's other companies like Zips out there that... all they do is intake'), positioning Zycus's embedded-AI breadth as the wedge. No named competitor is in active evaluation. The win path is to prove differentiated value of iAnalyze spend analytics, Merlin Intake (embedded in Teams, which Mandy confirmed is pervasive), and ANA negotiation versus the incumbent's idle capability.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-articulated and tied to a real compelling event. Owens & Minor was 'acquired by the relevant stakeholder in January', driving 'change... ways of working and the sense of urgency' and a mandate to 'revisit how we're doing things... be more nimble... reevaluate all of our agreements' (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026, Mandy Huff). Specific pains: rogue/maverick spend across 45 independently operating US DCs, weak spend visibility and taxonomy ('a lot of legacy, smaller suppliers'), and manual, underutilized contract management ('we can have all the technology we want, but if we're not using it'). The pain owner is Mandy as Head of Indirect Procurement, newly tasked since April 2026 with centralizing indirect spend.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process is at the earliest stage: an MNDA was sent on 11 Jun 2026 for review and execution and is still outstanding as of 30 Jun 2026 (SF Next Step note, 11 Jun 2026). Mandy explicitly flagged the absence of an NDA on the call as a reason to be 'careful with specifics' (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026), so executing the MNDA directly unblocks deeper discovery and competitive-intel sharing. No MSA, SOW, procurement channel, or InfoSec path has been discussed yet.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is not yet identified or engaged. Mandy Huff stated indirect orchestration/intake 'doesn't sit directly under my responsibility' but that she has 'aligned with my CPO' to have these conversations and make introductions (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). She referenced inviting 'potentially my CPO' to the Horizon conference. The CPO is unnamed on the call; Salesforce account contacts list both Emily Jessup (Chief Procurement Officer) and Rajesh Kumawat (VP & Chief Procurement Officer) but neither is confirmed on this opportunity. At a Platinum-Equity-owned enterprise a $300k+ platform purchase will require C-level sign-off, so identifying and meeting the CPO is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is undefined this early. The only process signal is Mandy's stated intent to 'have these conversations and have some introductions' and to 'loop others in' as conversations continue, plus alignment with her CPO (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). No procurement steps, approval chain, or timeline have been described. The call sequence so far is a 26 May intro (not recorded) and a 9 Jun discovery, with a solution-consultant-led demo agreed next. Mapping the decision and approval process is a near-term move once the demo establishes interest.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging, not yet formalized. Mandy said she wants 'to understand more about your orchestration' and the intake/Merlin agent, and that 'understanding a little bit more about your spend analytics' is the focus area she wants to pursue (Avoma intro call 9 Jun 2026). She flagged taxonomy help ('I've not worked for or consulted for a company that doesn't need help with their taxonomy'), contract-management automation, and tail-spend/negotiation on renewals. These map directly to the products in scope (iAnalyze spend analytics/Spinminer, Merlin Intake, ANA negotiation, taxonomy). No formal RFP or weighted criteria exist yet; the upcoming demo is the venue to confirm them.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage qualified deal moving on cadence: strong discovery call on 9 Jun 2026 (~3 weeks ago), follow-up demo being scheduled, close 243 days out on 28 Feb 2027.

flags: Competition intelligence gap: the incumbent S2P platform is unnamed, and no other spend-analytics or orchestration vendors have been surfaced. Mandy withheld details due to lack of NDA execution ('we don't have an NDA, so I wanna be careful with specifics'). Once the MNDA is signed, probe for the incumbent vendor, their module coverage, and adoption/satisfaction levels. If the incumbent offers spend analytics or intake, understand why Mandy is exploring Zycus (likely: weak AI, poor UX, low adoption post-acquisition). Position Zycus Merlin as the agentic AI differentiation and the 1,121 APIs as the integration safety net.

Quantify the win: Mandy articulated clear operational pain (45 DCs with rogue spend, poor taxonomy, decentralized indirect procurement post-Platinum acquisition) but no dollar savings target or ROI hurdle. The demo must include a sizing exercise: estimated tail-spend leakage across 45 DCs, potential savings from centralized contracts, and taxonomy cleanup ROI (e.g. X% spend now unclassified, Y hours/week manual cleanup burden). Arm Mandy with a one-page business case she can take to the CPO, anchored to Platinum's likely mandate for quick cost takeout post-acquisition.

Economic Buyer (CPO or VP Procurement) not identified or engaged. At a $9B+ healthcare distribution enterprise owned by the relevant stakeholder, a $300k+ platform purchase requires C-level or VP sign-off. Budget authority, approval process, and who controls commercial/pricing decisions are entirely unknown. Mandy mentioned aligning with her CPO but has not introduced them or discussed budget. Next move: during the demo call, ask Mandy to walk through the approval chain and invite the CPO to a follow-up commercial discussion.
No competitive intel: incumbent S2P platform and competitive evaluation status unknown. Blind to differentiation opportunities and win/loss drivers.

No quantified business case. Mandy articulated strong qualitative pain (spend visibility, taxonomy, rogue spend, post-acquisition centralization urgency) but provided no cost savings target, headcount impact, error rate, cycle time reduction, or ROI. Without a quantified case, executive sponsorship and budget approval are at risk. Next move: during the spend analytics demo, co-develop a savings estimate with Mandy using their DC-level spend data (e.g., 10% maverick spend reduction across $XM indirect spend = $YM savings) and position a pilot to validate ROI.
Convert the agreed demo into a quantified value story: pull O&M tail-spend/45-DC visibility pain into a savings hypothesis the CPO can fund, not a feature tour.
